item.title: People lay flowers at makeshift Khamenei memorial in Moscow
item.description: Residents gathered outside the Iranian Embassy in Moscow to lay flowers and pray at a makeshift memorial for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: Israelis celebrate Purim in bomb shelter amid missile alerts
item.description: Hundreds gathered in an underground parking shelter in Tel Aviv to celebrate Purim, as public events were restricted and missile alerts continued.

item.title: Workers clear rubble after US-Israeli strikes damage buildings in Tehran
item.description: Machinery removed debris from damaged buildings in Tehran after overnight US-Israeli strikes, as residents described fear during the attacks

item.title: Iraqi police fire tear gas at pro-Iran protest near US embassy in Baghdad
item.description: Police dispersed dozens of Iraqis protesting in support of Iran near Baghdad's Green Zone on Monday night.

item.title: Building collapses in southern Lebanon after Israeli strike targets Hezbollah-linked institution
item.description: A building collapses and smoke billows into the air as Israel targets offices of Al-Qard Al-Hassan, a Hezbollah-linked financial institution.

item.title: Tel Aviv residents assess damage after Iranian missile strike kills one
item.description: Residents in Tel Aviv surveyed damage after an Iranian missile strike killed one woman, as sirens and further alerts sent people back to shelters.

item.title: At least 31 killed as Israel launches strikes on Beirut after Hezbollah rocket fire
item.description: Israeli strikes hit Beirut and other parts of Lebanon after Hezbollah fired rockets and drones toward Israel, with Lebanon’s Health Ministry reporting at least 31 deaths.

item.title: Missile fire from Iran wounds five in Jerusalem
item.description: Five people were wounded in Jerusalem after missiles fired from Iran struck the area, as cross-border attacks continued for a third day.

item.title: Residents flee Beirut suburbs after Israeli strikes on Hezbollah
item.description: Residents fled Beirut’s southern suburbs after Israeli strikes targeted Hezbollah positions, as tensions linked to the Iran conflict spread to Lebanon.

item.title: Iranian missile strike on Beit Shemesh kills at least nine in Israel
item.description: At least nine people were killed after an Iranian missile struck a residential neighborhood in the Israeli city of Beit Shemesh.

item.title: Mourners gather in Mashhad after death of Iran’s Supreme Leader Khamenei
item.description: Mourners assembled at the Imam Reza Shrine in Mashhad after Iranian state media confirmed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in a US-Israeli airstrike.

item.title: Sirens sound in Jerusalem and Tehran after Israeli strikes on Iran
item.description: Air raid sirens and explosions were reported in Haifa, Jerusalem and Tehran after Israel launched what it described as coordinated strikes on Iran.

item.title: Civilians caught in crossfire as Pakistan–Afghan border clashes grow
item.description: Residents in Pakistan’s Bajaur district reported shells hitting civilian homes, with injured families taken to local hospitals as clashes spread across frontier areas.

item.title: Pakistan and Afghanistan trade border fire as airstrikes hit cities
item.description: Pakistan is facing renewed fighting along its border with Afghanistan as clashes continue near the Torkham crossing.

item.title: Angèle returns to Brussels stage after four-year hiatus
item.description: Belgian singer Angèle reunited with fans in Brussels, premiering a new music video after four years without releases, drawing thousands and sparking excitement online.

item.title: Brazil races to find survivors after deadly Minas Gerais floods
item.description: For a third consecutive day, rescue teams in Minas Gerais continued searching through mud and debris after landslides killed at least 54 people and displaced thousands amid forecasts of further rainfall.

item.title: Ramadan in Gaza marked by grief amid fragile ceasefire and ruins
item.description: Ramadan in Gaza unfolds amid mourning, destroyed homes, and mass displacement, while UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk warns of long-term consequences.

item.title: Therian trend: Masked youth identifying as animals gather in Buenos Aires
item.description: Teenagers in Buenos Aires identifying as “therians” are going viral on social media, prompting mixed public reactions from mental health professionals.

item.title: Peru under state of emergency after El Niño floods hit Arequipa
item.description: Flooding tied to El Niño Costero devastates Peru, killing dozens and damaging hundreds of kilometres of roads as a nationwide emergency speeds relief.

item.title: Russian overnight attacks pound Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, dozens hurt
item.description: Russian overnight drone and missile attacks struck Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, injuring civilians, damaging apartment blocks and cutting heating to hundreds of homes.

item.title: Japan tests AI robot monk ‘Buddharoid’ to guide Buddhist faithful
item.description: An AI powered robot monk called Buddharoid debuts in a Kyoto temple, answering personal questions and raising new questions about faith and technology.

item.title: Floods kill 46 as mud and debris choke Brazilian towns in Minas Gerais State
item.description: Brazil’s Minas Gerais reels after torrential rains cause deadly floods and landslides, killing at least 46, displacing thousands and destroying businesses.

item.title: Jordan: Prince Harry and Meghan visit Gaza evacuees in WHO mental health mission
item.description: Jordan welcomed Prince Harry and Meghan, on Wednesday as they met patients evacuated from Gaza during a humanitarian visit focused on health and recovery.

item.title: Merz tours Forbidden City during key China trade discussions
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz continued his first official visit to China with a tour of Beijing’s Forbidden City, as Berlin pushes for a more balanced trade relationship amid growing economic tensions.

item.title: Iran faces renewed student protests in Tehran as new semester begins
item.description: Renewed student protests erupt at Tehran universities as a new semester begins, reviving slogans from recent unrest and highlighting growing tensions.

item.title: Germany launches Ukraine war museum in Berlin
item.description: Germany opens the first museum outside Ukraine on Russia’s full-scale war, with drones, missile fragments and testimonies at Berlin Story Bunker.

item.title: Watch: Seagull rescued with CPR by Istanbul football captain after ball collision
item.description: Turkey witnessed an unusual scene during an amateur playoff in Istanbul when a seagull was hit by a clearance kick.

item.title: Brazil landslides: 43 missing after heavy rain hits state of Minas Gerais
item.description: Brazil is facing deadly floods in Minas Gerais after record rain hit Juiz de Fora and Uba.

item.title: Poland marks four years since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine in Warsaw
item.description: Crowds in Warsaw marked four years since Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, demanding continued Western support and affirming that Crimea is Ukraine.

item.title: Trump welcomes Olympic medal-winning hockey team during State of the Union address
item.description: US President Donald Trump welcomes the Olympic medal-winning men's hockey team during his State of the Union address at the Capitol.

item.title: Zelensky and EU leaders honour war dead as Ukraine marks four years
item.description: Ukraine marks four years since Russia’s invasion with a Kyiv memorial as Zelensky and EU leaders honour the dead and vow to keep resisting.

item.title: Kyiv region mourns four years of invasion as battles rage in the east
item.description: Residents in Irpin and Bucha light candles for victims four years after Russia’s invasion, while Zelensky says Moscow has not broken Ukraine and vows a fair peace.

item.title: Flour war paints Greek town Galaxidi in colour for clean Monday
item.description: Greece marks Clean Monday with Galaxidi’s flour war, as crowds throw dyed flour to close the carnival season.

item.title: Basel carnival lampoons Donald Trump with satirical glowing lanterns
item.description: Basel’s 700-year-old carnival begins with the 4am Morgenstreich, as lights go out and masked musicians parade with glowing, satirical lanterns.

item.title: New York buried in nearly 60 centimetres of snow as Manhattan falls silent
item.description: New York was blanketed by nearly 60 centimetres of snow, shutting schools, silencing traffic and turning Central Park into a playground of sledges and snowmen.

item.title: Von der Leyen in Kyiv as Ukraine marks four years of Russia’s war
item.description: Ukraine marks four years since Russia’s invasion as Ursula von der Leyen visits Kyiv, vowing sustained EU financial and military support.

item.title: Georgia village revives Berikaoba, an ancient pagan spring festival
item.description: Each spring, Georgia’s village of Didi Chailuri revives Berikaoba, a pagan rite with masked berikas, music and wrestling, hoping for fertility and good harvest.

item.title: Visitors take in snowy NYC ahead of traffic closure
item.description: A major blizzard sweeps the US Northeast, closing schools, cancelling flights and shutting nonessential traffic in New York as millions shelter indoors.

item.title: Verona protesters say Italy’s winter Olympics waste public money
item.description: Activists in Verona marched on Sunday before the Milan Cortina 2026 Winter Olympics closing ceremony, questioning billions spent on venues and doubting claims of renewable power and long-term benefits.

item.title: Deadly Russian strikes pound Kyiv suburb and energy sites across Ukraine
item.description: Russia launched massive overnight strikes on Ukraine Saturday, hitting a Kyiv suburb, killing one and injuring at least a dozen including children as Zelenskyy condemned Moscow’s attacks.

item.title: Ukraine probes deadly Lviv blasts that killed police officer
item.description: Ukraine probes twin blasts in Lviv that killed a young policewoman and injured dozens, as Zelenskyy reports arrests and Russia is suspected.

item.title: Fans flock to Japan zoo to see viral baby monkey Punch
item.description: The baby monkey, named "Punch", has drawn attention online and among visitors who have come to see him in person.

item.title: First Friday of Ramadan sees mass prayers at al Aqsa and shattered Gaza
item.description: Tens of thousands pray at Jerusalem's Al-Aqsa Mosque under heavy Israeli security as Ramadan begins, while families in Gaza mark the holy month among rubble and scarce aid.

item.title: From Milano Cortina to Dakar 2026: Olympic mascots Tina and Ayo share the stage
item.description: Italy saw mascot mania in Milan as Tina, face of Milano Cortina 2026, welcomed Ayo from Dakar 2026.

item.title: France on edge as Loire floods and storm Pedro threatens more chaos
item.description: France is watching the Loire spill across streets and car parks in Les Ponts-de-Cé, near Angers.

item.title: China celebrates Spring Festival with lion dances at Great Wall of China
item.description: China marked the Spring Festival with lion and dragon dances at the Badaling Great Wall on 19 February 2026. The shows formed part of New Year celebrations linked to the Year of the Fire Horse.

item.title: Western France floods deepen after 35 days of rain
item.description: After 35 days of rain, France battles major floods, evacuations and power cuts while forecasters say rivers will keep rising despite drier weather ahead.

item.title: Humanoid robots dance into Beijing’s Lunar New Year celebrations
item.description: Robots dance at a Beijing mall’s Lunar New Year show, dazzling visitors with smoother, human-like moves that highlight China’s tech-driven celebrations.

item.title: Snowfall in Frankfurt disrupts flights at Germany’s largest airport
item.description: Snow fell across Frankfurt as Germany’s busiest airport reported dozens of flight cancellations and delays due to winter weather.

item.title: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un drives rocket launcher vehicle
item.description: Ahead of a key party congress, Kim showcased rocket launchers and a housing project in Pyongyang.
